rationale: Ground truth is trivial (bot rate-limit notice + a bare 'lgtm' approval), so recall is moot for both; the real differentiator is precision and groundedness. X correctly identifies the core rationale (upstream rename alignment), verifies against the actual upstream checkout with concrete line references, confirms zero stragglers, and lands on APPROVE — matching the real reviewer's verdict exactly. Y does equally solid hunk-by-hunk validation and independently surfaces the same latent-AttributeError insight, but pads the review with two speculative 'nit' suggestions (documenting an intentional upstream-matching name, and testing a pre-existing gap unrelated to this diff) that read as manufactured scope creep on a PR that genuinely needed no changes, and its COMMENT verdict diverges from the actual clean approval. Y's suggestions are more concretely actionable (file/line plus explicit asks), giving it an edge on actionability, but X's tighter, verification-backed, correctly-calibrated review is the more valuable read overall.
